āpajjīti.example_1: “Venerable, don’t socialize with families too often.”

nigacchi.sutta_1: so tatonidānaṃ maraṇampi nigacchi maraṇamattampi dukkhaṃ.
nigacchi.example_1: resulting in death and deadly pain.

vāto.example_1: And the cool breeze still blows on it.

appakampi.sutta_1: na ca no amhesu appakampi kataṃ nassissatī’ti.
appakampi.example_1: We won’t forget even a small thing done for us.’

tissaṃ.sutta_1: atha kho āyasmā tisso bhagavato pitucchāputto yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṃ abhivādetvā ekamantaṃ nisīdi dukkhī dummano assūni pavattayamāno. atha kho bhagavā āyasmantaṃ tissaṃ etadavoca:
tissaṃ.example_1: Then Venerable Tissa—the Buddha’s cousin on his father’s side—went to the Buddha, bowed, and sat down to one side. He was miserable and sad, with tears flowing. Then the Buddha said to him:

tanukaṃ.sutta_1: “passatha no tumhe, bhikkhave, etaṃ bhikkhuṃ āgacchantaṃ odātakaṃ tanukaṃ tuṅganāsikan”ti?
tanukaṃ.example_1: “Mendicants, do you see that monk coming—white, thin, with a pointy nose?”
